definition: The events that will necessarily happen to a particular person or thing in the future.
example: Destiny is not only what happens to us; all we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given us.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring

example: The world is indeed full of peril, and in it there are many dark places; but still there is much that is fair...
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers

definition: The ability to do something that frightens one; bravery.
example: Courage is found in unlikely places.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers

definition: The ability to make considered decisions or come to sensible conclusions.
example: Many that live deserve death. And some that die deserve life. Can you give it to them? Then do not be too eager to deal out death in judgement.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring

definition: Deep sorrow, especially that caused by someone's death.
example: Grief need not be denied: I will not say do not weep, for not all tears are an evil.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King

definition: The ability or capacity to do something or act in a particular way.
example: There is only one Lord of the Ring, only one who can bend it to his will. And he does not share power.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring

definition: A feeling of expectation and desire for a certain thing to happen.
example: Hope remains even when the world is full of peril and many dark places—still there is much that is fair.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers

definition: The capacity of an individual to act independently and to make their own free choices.
example: Agency is the choice before us: all we have to decide is what to do with the time that is given us.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring

definition: Friendly association, especially with people who share interests; a company of companions.
example: Fellowship is the answer when the road is too dark for one pair of feet.

definition: A dark area; metaphorically, evil or despair that follows light.
example: Even the smallest person can change the course of the future when the shadow seems complete.

definition: The feeling of sorrow and compassion caused by the suffering of others.
example: It was pity that stayed Bilbo’s hand—and pity that left a crack for light to enter later.

definition: A person who travels aimlessly; one who roams.
example: Not all those who wander are lost—a wanderer may still walk a true road.

definition: To light or set on fire; to arouse or inspire an emotion.
example: A single act of kindness can kindle hope when strategy has gone cold.

definition: The ability to withstand hardship over a long period.
example: Endurance, not glory, carries the Ring when strength has already been spent.

definition: A firmly held belief or opinion.
example: Conviction without evidence is noise; conviction after struggle is leadership.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring

definition: The quality of being coherent and intelligible.
example: Clarity is kindness in speech—confusion is often a way to hide.
source: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
